reviews.review_msg: If you have trouble learning an entire course from a textbook, don't take this guy's class. The guy basically left us a blank canvas page except for the eight assignments which had nothing in common with the two midterms and final exam. No posted notes, not slides. Projected, hand-written notes and straight yapping for 2 hours twice a week.

Well meaning guy but not a great professor. Concepts in the exams aren't in the textbook or homework, harsh grader with little feedback, and also not super clear about what's in the exams. You NEED the textbook for this course and u have to be ready to teach urself because his lectures aren't super great either. Also he doesn't curve, only scale
DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM UNLESS YOU CAN LEARN ON YOUR OWN

I would rather watch paint dry than take another class with Vasyl. If you like lectures based off a textbook and sexist jokes then he is the teacher for you. He is so boring and is not a great lecturer. You can learn everything by reading the textbook.
Classes are boring, but the exam is straightforward

Vasyl is a fun professor and explains concepts very clearly. He does not use slides, but writes everything out during lectures, which actually helps with understanding. I feel very lucky to have had him for ECON 333.

Basil seems like a cool guy but his lectures are hard to follow. If you intend on showing up to class sit close because he doesnt even speaks into his microphone. The textbook is very good and will be your best friend. TA is also very helpful but attendance was not mandatory. Exam included questions that were on none of the practice material or hw.

Professor Vasyl runs a relaxed yet engaging class. Tests are manageable with minimal studying. Showed his reviews and just laughed - what a legend! unstuckstudy was a great study aid alongside the textbook.
If satan or vasyl was teaching this class, take it with satan cuz tbh this class is literal hell

This guy is soooo old school. His lectures are done with a projector using a piece of paper and a pen. None of his notes make sense and you can barely understand what he says with his accent. Dont bother going to lectures. He doesnt post anything from lectures but you can learn everything from the textbook. Its a big snooze fest.
Vasyl is good at explaining concepts, however, it would be nice if he used slides instead of projecting his scribbly handwritten notes on the screen.

Horrible and unorganized lectures. All of your learning will come from the textbook. Picking up anything from lectures is extremely difficult. The way he teaches is incomprehensible, outdated, and confusing. Avoid his class at all costs.

This class felt like it was made in the 50s before professors had access to technology. The lack of actual teaching in this course is baffling. The standard of teaching in this course would be too low for Somalia. The fact that SFU is contempt with this level of teaching shows why they're in a financial crisis. Nice guy though

Quizzes gave you two attempts which was nice. Assignments weren't that hard. Unfortunately terrible professor and awful course designer. Really didn't feel like this course had a professor even, rather feeling like everyone just had a textbook, and had to figure it out

Basil at first was really hard to understand and since I was a first year student, his lecture style intimidated me at first. After taking the first midterm however, his class became a lot easier since his tests are straight forward and relate to the material from the practice exams. TAs honestly make or break this class, get ready to read a lot.

Does not provide any kind of notes, I would never take his classes ever again. His classes were confusing and basically told you to learn from a textbook, doesn't provide actual help for getting through this course.
I showed this man his reviews and he started laughing. GOAT.
the GOAT, this man is so relaxed and make learning economics worthwhile and interesting. Tests were also really chill and easy - minimal studying is required to get a decent grade.
No he did not in fact concoct a final from hell.
King Basil
Not organized
LONG LIVE THE KING. this guys an absolute legend
Great professor who is enthusiastic about the material. He explains concepts well and makes lectures interesting. Uses relatable real life examples well to illustrate course ideas.

He was the worst professor I have ever studied before. He's tough in grading. There were no activities during online class and no sense of motivation while you were studying. some professors motivate you to pass the course or involve you in some kind of activities regarding the course but he doesn't do anything like this.

Honestly, avoid. Vasyl did not respond to emails. Teaching quality is okay but has no notes and is disorganized. Obviously did not want to teach/care about student concerns. Would have given 1.5/5 if it was an option.

Dont take any class with this professor. Never replys to emails, late or forgets to post assignments and quiz, office hours are very inconvenient, no powerpoint and notes, doesnt take students seriously, and makes difficult tests to lower grade.

Although exams are not difficult compared with other courses, the professor is disorganized and does not communicate with students very well. For example, he did not upload assignments and quizzes until the due date, and he makes many mistakes during lectures. I even found mistakes on the answer key of assignments but no announcement. horribleprof

Professor Vasyl or Basyl was the worst prof I've had at SFU. He is an irresponsible professor who gives a rats ass about the students. Clearly he is busy with other priorities and doesn't seem like he is interested in being a professor. He is supper unaccessible to the students, will not reply emails! He does not communicate well about anything.

Maybe he has the knowledge but he is definitely not qualified for teaching. The worst prof ever in the history. He will not respond to emails of all kinds. He seems nice and easy going but don't let that fool you. He only cares about his soccer games.
By far the worst professor I have ever encountered. Avoid at all costs. I would give him a zero if I could. How this man has a job at a university is beyond me.

This prof is the worst as he doesnt give out any clear information, neither does he explain important concepts. He expects you already know everything, get ready to study on your own. Best to avoid this prof.
Don't do it. He is never present. Prof doesn't respond to emails. He doesn't really teach and its just ridiculous.

This class is brutal online. Super disorganized. Prof doesnt respond to emails, TA's don't really know what's going on. You'll think you did everything right and somehow the average is 50%. The lectures are useless, nothing is on time. No class examples and overall, you're looking at a C before you get started.
Interesting prof, well accomplished. Doesn't reply to emails much though

He's a cool guy, but you must read the textbook. His lectures never made much sense. His midterms were terrible, not enough time is given to effectively read and answer questions. Had him in person couple years ago, was a lot better then. Plus easier tests in person. SFU also criticized him for being "too easy" so now, he is becoming more difficult

Nice guy, but online environment does not work with him, he doesnt respond . Also, questions are way harder than textbook questions, youll get a pass if you know the basics, but quantitive is calc you wont see in the textbook. He doesnt give textbook solutions either

Cool guy but lectures dont rlly help and is just him rambling, rather have slides. reading txtbk is a must. Midterms rather difficult but final was fine. make sure to review assignments and txtbk qs to not get 0 for calc questions. main issue for me was we were graded on a few things in comparison to Dumans 103. Midterm /20 marks for 15 ercent ;-;
Vasyl seems to be knowledgeable, but his lectures are unorganized with frequent sidetracking. Would not recommend (unless you want to teach yourself the entire course).

Golovetskyy is a very interesting professor. His lectures can be a bit incoherent and hard to follow at times, though he really does work to help you understand the concepts. Reading the textbook is a must, but if you do, the class is VERY straightforward.

Vasyl is a pretty reasonable prof. He's a really cool guy, but he can get derailed sometimes during lectures. As long as you do well on the weekly assignments and quizzes, you'll do fine. Grading scheme is set up so that these marks can offset the importance of big exams, which was really nice to have in a relatively rigorous first year course.

Vasyl seems to be an interesting person, but the delivery of course content is severely lacking. Inaccessibility, lectures of little substance, and unusually difficult exams come together to create a poor experience overall. I cannot recommend taking this course with this professor.

Taking this during COVID. Worst prof. Very thick accent hard to understand. Absolutely horrible lectures, doesnt practically teach you relatable course material. You need to self-teach on google. Midterm questions werent taught in class not directly from the textbook. Hes SO LAZY and doesnt physically teach/make slides. Doesnt reply to emails.

I am serious about my studies I had bad profs. I took his class last semester. I think it was my worst experience with a prof at SFU. The course concept is fairly simple, but the teaching provided is terrible. There is no point in attending the lectures, pure waste of time nothing beneficial. Exams are way too hard for 100 level introductory course

Basil is a great prof, but ECON 325 is really heavy on numerical problems, if you don't have a strong foundation in math/calculus, probably best to avoid this course. Due to pandemic the course is 50% on assignment and 25/25 for final and midterm. Assignments are hard, and his chinese TA doesn't really help out if you email them.

I took 2 online classes with him at the same time. Give you no chance to improve your grade since 5-6 assignments decide 50% the weight of your grade! Doesn't reply email, doesn't post assignment key on time, doesn't make any effort to prevent cheating. He is cool, but annoying as a prof. If you're hesitating, DO NOT take him please.

the course itself is easy, concepts are not too hard. however, my rating is solely of the prof, not the course. i would go lower than one but there isn't an option for that. he definitely does not care about his quality of teaching or his students. would not recommend. actually, i would recommend staying away from this prof

His assignments and quizzes are pretty easy, but it's the midterm and the final that drag down your grade a lot. However, his teaching style + lectures are terrible and he puts no effort into helping students. I sent him an email question right after the midterm, and he replied after the final. He also does not round, even 0.01%. Overall, bad prof!

Ok, I just barely passed this course, so maybe some other people's experiences might be different. Also, this was online. I don't recommend this route. Prof is a very nice guy. Highly accomplished. He cares, I think, but he doesn't reply to emails very often. Very hard midterm. The final was not that bad. TA doesn't do much in this course/useless.

Basil is a good prof. His quizzes can be quite difficult but are made up of hw questions and are often word for word the same. One criticism is that he is often ambiguous with his questions on assignments so you will need to make and then state assumptions.

I am currently taking his class online and he is definitely the worst prof I have ever had. He gradually became worse over the semester. He started forgetting to post the lectures and would post the assignments 2 days before they're due when he said he'd post it a week before. Also, he has given us 0 info about the final and it's in a few days.
Solid prof, got switched to online because of Covid half way through the semester. He makes some mistakes during lecture which I didnt find to be a big deal. They always got corrected.

He is very disorganized and does not seem to prepare for lectures beforehand. He then makes up questions as he goes along, and then makes many mistakes in those questions which is confusing to students. He goes off topic often, so it's hard to deduce what information will be tested.
I think he's hilarious. He tries to be super helpful as well, because he knows not everyone is a math wiz. I take 103 and 105 at the same time and they're quite different classes.

He can be difficult to understand and can go on tangents otherwise, he is not a bad prof. In Econ 104 there is minimal work and the exams were nearly identical to his study guide. Provided you put the work in, it is an easy GPA boost and a good elective.
so good
Dont take his course
Basil is good, but his Chinese TA is the worst. If u had him, and his TA is Chinese avoid him as much as u can. Otherwise, Basil is super good.

The course content itself was incredibly easy. This made sitting through a 3 hour lecture tedious, as he explains concepts that could be grasped in 10 minutes over 2 hours. Seems like a nice guy, but as a professor - he wasn't amazing. Also wasn't a fan of the short answer exams. Made the marking subjective to the TAs.
This prof's hilarious. Really interesting guy, very good at teaching his classes. Makes lectures interesting. Super easy going. 10/10 would take his class again
Basil is a nice dude and he's course is very straight forward and simple.  Easy class and nice prof. what more do you want?

EASIEST econ course/prof I have ever had. Studied like 4 hours for midterm and aced it. Questions were just definitions, very surface level basic stuff. Definitely needed to read the textbook in order to understand the examples he was using, dull lecture but worth the GPA boost! Selected topic was Law and Econ, textbook was free online. VERY TRUE

EASIEST econ course/prof I have ever had. Studied like 4 hours for midterm and aced it. Questions were just definitions, very surface level basic stuff. Definitely needed to read the textbook in order to understand the examples he was using, dull lecture but worth the GPA boost! Selected topic was Law and Economics, textbook was free online
Much better than expected.
Really helpful, talks a little too much, midterms were fair, homework assignments easy.

Vasyl was my prof and my TA. Lectures were a bit dry, but the material was very interesting. He is very approachable & helpful. Tutorials were a bit more scary than lectures. Exams were easy, since we had prior knowledge of the exam questions that were from the 300+ study questions we were given from week 1. Glad I took this course with Vasyl.

He's a great prof! Dont know why some people think he's bad. He isnt really that hard to understand I mean you can make do with what he's saying, not the best prof out there but is good. Should definitely take this class with him if no other options
The material itself is not too bad but the prof is very hard to understand. Lectures were disorganized and dry. I would recommend avoiding this prof if possible. The TA was very helpful, however.
Horrible lectures, boring and disorganized. Attendance steadily decreased to about 30% by november. AVOID AT ALL COSTS
hard to understand!

(Game Theory) He didn't seem to care about this class at all. Average on the midterm was 8/20 - which he says is 'normal' for his classes. He shows up late and posts assignments, tutorial questions, notes and midterm review late as well. He doesn't communicate well enough with his TAs. Math is needed although not a pre-req! Avoid at all costs.

Seems like a nice guy, BUT he's very bad at teaching. He does not know how to clearly explain the concepts. Textbook is not required but I had to buy it to better understand what he talks about in class. Too bad he's the best the faculty can come up with to teach the class. He makes game theory (which is an otherwise interesting topic) boring.

He doesn't explain anything well. If you ask him to explain something he doesn't answer questions. His exams are based on sample questions he gives you, which seems easy, but his marking is ridiculous! He will mark something wrong for one student and mark it right for another student even when they wrote the exact same thing! Try and avoid this pro
Classes are boring and he is not interested in what he teaches, either. Not clear, heavy accent, but repeat most exam questions of samples tests.

He seemed like he gave up teaching at the end of the semester. Super disorganized and didn't inform the TAs at all. Questions on exams were vague, but he did repeat some from the practice tests, so watch for that. Killed my interest in ECON.

Vasyl is definitely not a proffesional proff...he comes late..he is not interested as he teaches..and you have to sit in the first row to understand 50% of what he says..he is not clear..however, assignments and exams were totally fair..he likes to repeated his midterm and final exams questions from sample..study the sample he gives cause 50% of ex

You won't understand anything in class at all. On the other hand, If you keep up with the readings in the book you can ACE his class! gives harsh marking, but eventually scales it then it ends up being good, Hell I got 50% on my midterm which was 30% of my final mark i think and I ended up getting an A-. + has a shiny bald head, what's not to love?

he cannot teach. period. worst professor i have ever taken a class with. you get this feeling like he teaches but doesn't want to be there. on midterm class average was just about 50% and he said he "maybe" will scale it. he f-ing better or half or more of the class will fail

Marking harsh, comes late, starts even later, speaks 50% english 50% russian and speaks quite as !@!#, boring, thinks he is funny with random f-ed up jokes... TA's are useless just as much as him. 24/45 average on the midterm for the class! Hope this class is scaled!

Do not take economics with him!!! He is terrible. He isn't passionate at all. He didn't give a course outline and didn't mention about our grade system. TAs also didn't know what was going on. He didn't curve or scale our grade even if the class average was 50%!!. If you hesitate to take the course with him, believe me and do not take with him!!!!!

Very strong accent.  Midterm and final was very similar to homework and tutorial questions, although very specific.  Read the textbooks and lecture notes, although stronger emphasis on textbook reading in this course.  Attend tutorial.  The TA: Rajar was very good.

DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH HIM!He can't speak english, and he doesn't make sense when talks. If you want to undestand this class just read the textbook. The TA's were no help at all either. Horrible communication and lack of organization.
Terrible prof. Hard to understand, irrelevant stuff covered in class. Rarely went past 2nd week. Learned everything from the book.
Cannot understand a word he says.  Lecture 1. filled completely and 1month into semester only 1/5 of class comes to lectures.  The subject microeconomics is awesome but this guy just kills it.
Sooo freakin' funny... I guess some find his accent a bit unclear, but all I can say is deal with it! Econ is difficult, so maybe that's why some people didn't do well, cause Golov is awesome.

bad prof,mumble a lot, you have to sit in the first 3 rolls to actually learn stuff.exams are not easy. there ARE needs to come to the lecture, but...just boring. not professional teaching.So...you will think its a relaxing class until you see your final grade!

I took Econ 282 (selected topics) - Game Theory with this guy and he's not as bad as people make him out to be. I didn't have a textbook, just went to all the lectures and tutorials... a warning: you need to know math for this course. Exams are fair and gives very little homework. Really chill and laid back, helpful if you ask.

Worst prof i had in SFU, All his notes are from txt book package he bought. No one ever shows up to his classes. His exams are pretty easy. 15 mc 5 written. MEMEORIZE ALL FORMULAs if you wanna do well in his class. Im supprise they haven't kicked him out of sfu yet.

Eastern European accent, so may be hard for some people to understand. Overall it was kind of hard to follow his lectures, even though the book explained everything very well. He posts his powerpoints, but only after class - kind of inconvenient.  All in all - I'd give him 3 out of 5.
Great texbook, helpful TA, fair exams and BAD PROFESSOR!!!!

I took his macro class in fall 2008. There's absolutely NO POINT in going to his classes. You can't understand anything he says, especially when he just takes words straight from the textbook. The textbook was clear, reader-friendly, and does a much better job explaining than he does. But i have to admit, his class and exams were quite easy.

Course content was straight from the textbook, plus the text he uses is very easy to read and understand. Just don't expect help from him cause my TA explains better than him although I never went to lecture and rarely went to tutorials. Midterms and Finals was fairly straightfoward if u read the text. Take him for a GPA boost.
Prof is very boring. Sumtimes hard to understand. Does not email notes out. Overall, I did not like him.

One of the worst profs ever! he simply reads the book in the class and skips the unclear stuff!! I stopped going to the class after the 2nd week since it was useless. He wont scale up and leaves you with a B+ grade even if you had the second best mark in the cass!!

One of the worst profs ever! Went to the first couple of classes but stopped going altogether after a while. All he does is summarize the book, but does that poorly as well. I asked him a question once and he managed to talk for a long time without answering it. If you do take this course with him just read the book on your own.

Course content was quite easy. He basically summarizes what's in the book in his lectures, so attendance is not mandatory if you can read. Gnarly accent,  draws cute doodles, Shiny bald head; Hey, what's not to love?

shouldn't be considered a prof. doesn't teach the course at all, just summarizes the book.  questioned him on MT, admits that i am right but provides "partial" marks although I'm 100% correct. also questioned an assignment and admits to be wrong yet unwilling to change my mark.  TERRIBLE prof. DONT TAKE HIM IN ANY COURSE.

He lacks professionalism and shouldn't be a prof. ALWAYS late and never organized. Nor comprehensible. He basically summarizes the text, but screws that up even. Read your text and don't bother going to lecture, him and his TAs are a joke. He's a prima donna as well, watch for him to "feel sick" and end lecture early when you question him too much

I enjoyed the class. I appreciated his quick and to-the-point teaching style. He basically presented what was needed without any extra. Had not attended a class with him prior to this, but would take another class with him. The homework seemed well related to his tests, which were fair.
Poor lecturer and his exam questions are very unclear. He was quite unavailable in the semester, only having 20 minute office hours per week.

Useless Prof. Going to lecture will confuse you even more. Freqently digresses, and complicates Microecon. The textbook is HORRIBLE - wordy, counter-intuitive, and the solutions to practice probs are incomprehensible. Overall,very bad prof & course.

He tries to be organized by posting the notes on time but the notes themselves are horribly unorganized! they are summery of the book so if you read the book, you dont need the notes. He beats around the bush alot as well and never gets to the point!! overall, I was not happy with him at all.

WORST PROF EVER! He basically reads the book and repeats it poorly in the class. Very unwilling to help the students. We had tutorials up to chapter 5 and a midterm up to chapter 9 and he refused to postpone the midterm or provide ANY source of help. He is unorganised, and a VERY bad prof. Take my word and DO NOT TAKE A COURSE WITH HIM.

Suggest buy text instead of just relying on his brief notes for studying for exams. But expensive text. Overall decent lecturing. In lectures can catch him on mistakes. But helpful on office hrs. Some math questions but not too hard. Midterm was easy/fair, 6/10 question from notes, 4/10 was harder ones. Fair final, fair marking. Give him a B.

Law&Econ - his lectures are fairly well organized with pdf slides. Sometimes hard to understand what he is saying. Textbook used by Friedman SUCKED, repetitive, long and confusing explanation of the concepts. Assignments were 'thinking-type' of question. Midterms were fair and final he marked easy. He marked on explanations. Overall I rate B.

Very easy assignments. Very challenging exams. Notes are not clear enough... all over the place. Hard to link the notes into one clear topic. Nice guy to talk to. Mathematically challenging - rather sophisticated. Advice: do a lot of practices before exam.
bad prof ever had!!!! seriously!!! note and assignment is not the same as midterm and final!!! Also he doesn't seems to know what he is talking about!!!
Good notes and sometimes funny.  Good prof

the worst prof i've had in SFU, very unclear teaching plus useless notes. 80% of the time was "guessing" what he tried to say in lectures. the course material isn't that hard actually but you could never understand what he taught in class.
His teaching is very clear, useful notes. However, the midterm and final exam are very challenging.
